symptoms.symptom_name: Tremor and palpitations after receiving the pathology alert

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catecholamine-driven tremor and palpitations are typical of an acute anxiety response.
Tachycardia and tremor are non-specific and do not by themselves distinguish anxiety from early sepsis.
The absence of violent, uncontrollable rigors makes bacteraemic sepsis less likely, though it does not exclude it.
A shivery, on-edge feeling without teeth-chattering is consistent with sympathetic arousal.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptom onset locked to reading the abnormal result, worsening with rumination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tight temporal lock to the triggering information and escalation with checking behaviour is characteristic of a health-anxiety reaction.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Relying on the subjective 'feverish' feeling leaves febrile neutropenia neither confirmed nor excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An objectively normal temperature is the single most useful patient-obtainable datum against febrile neutropenia at this moment — but it is a snapshot and can change.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic stable low white-cell count recalled from prior testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A previously-noted, stable low count reframes tonight's flag as a known chronic trait rather than an acute drop.
A long-standing stable count argues against acute marrow failure.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Confirmatory of the autoimmune mechanism; a specialist/laboratory test, never available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive anti-neutrophil antibodies establish the autoimmune basis of the chronic neutropenia — a sealed clinician/laboratory result.

investigation_details.expected_result: Mild chronic neutropenia (1.0-1.5 x10^9/L); no other cytopenia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The precise count and its stability cannot be known via telehealth; a GP repeat FBC is required to confirm the benign trait.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild neutropenia (not profound <0.5) with no other cytopenia fits a benign trait and carries low severe-infection risk.
Mild rather than profound neutropenia lowers, though does not remove, febrile-neutropenia risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bruising, petechiae, bleeding gums, mouth ulcers or bone pain (patient-observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding/bruising and mucosal or bone-pain signs argues strongly against acute marrow failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lymphadenopathy or organomegaly on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of nodes or organomegaly argues against a haematological malignancy — but requires palpation, so it is a sealed clinician finding unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No localising infective focus (no cough, sore throat, dysuria, wound)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No identifiable source of infection lowers the probability of an evolving neutropenic sepsis.
No coryza, sore throat or cough makes a viral cause of transient neutropenia unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heart rate around 100-105 on a consumer wearable, well-perfused

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild sinus tachycardia fits sympathetic arousal; as a non-specific, unverified wearable reading it must not be over-weighted in either direction.

symptoms.symptom_name: No myelosuppressive medication, no recent chemotherapy or viral illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excluding drug-induced and post-viral neutropenia strengthens the benign autoimmune explanation for a chronic low count.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Sympathetic anxiety cluster locked to the pathology alert
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tremor, palpitations, subjective chills and mild tachycardia with onset tied to reading the result and worsened by rumination form a coherent acute anxiety picture.
Afebrile, chronic stable count, no marrow-failure signs, no infective focus and no secondary cause together support a benign trait — but only once each has been actively elicited.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most useful patient-obtainable datum to appraise febrile neutropenia now; must be actively requested rather than inferred from the subjective feeling.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat full blood count with differential and blood film (via GP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the neutrophil count is stable and isolated, and formally characterises the benign trait; appropriate within a week for a well, afebrile patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent emergency-department bloods with empirical broad-spectrum IV antibiotics
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for a true febrile neutropenia or a septic-appearing patient. For this afebrile, well-perfused patient with no focus it is over-investigation and over-treatment — the over-triage error the case probes.
